setup.trigger: Swept True London Open (29,397.5) + PSP confluence (29,395-29,415)
setup.entry_reason: OTE retracement after London Open sweep + bearish displacement

management.notes: Clean entry, TP1 hit. Should have taken partial and let run to 29,175 PSP cluster for 4R+

trades.lesson: When setup scores 8/9 and TP1 hits, move SL to BE and let 50% run to TP2. Left +2R extra on the table.

setup.trigger: Rejection at True NY Open (29,275) + MES1! resistance
setup.entry_reason: OTE retracement short after True NY Open rejection

management.notes: Reached 1.5R profit (29,200) but no BE set. Price reversed via Stop Hunt to 29,288 and hit SL.
management.mistake: Did not move SL to BE when price reached 1.5R. Cost: -1R unnecessary loss.

trades.lesson: CRITICAL: When price reaches 1R+ in profit, move SL to BE immediately. This trade was a winner that became a loser due to missing BE management. PSP Cluster 29,175 was strong support — should have been flagged as potential reversal zone.

setup.trigger: Sellside Liquidity sweep at PSP 29,175 + NY AM reversal
setup.entry_reason: Pullback to True NY Open (29,275) after strong reversal impulse

management.notes: Reached TP1 (29,302) and PSP (29,314). Should have closed 35% there. Price pulled back below 29,275 triggering BE exit. Exited on 5-min close below 29,275 — correct decision.
management.good_decision: Used 5-min close below key level as exit signal. Prevented loss.

trades.lesson: Counter-trend trades (against daily bias) need faster partial exits. When TP1 hits on a counter-trend trade, close 50% immediately. Bias switched back to Bearish within minutes — always respect the bigger bias.

setup.trigger: Limit short at PSP 29,665.5 — second test after London sweep
setup.entry_reason: PSP resistance + 4H/1H Bearish bias + NY AM Kill Zone + second PSP test
confluence: Second test of PSP (first swept in London)

management.notes: Price swept PSP to 29,670, continued to 29,718. SL at 29,712.5 hit. PSP was broken — bullish momentum stronger than 1H/4H bias.
management.mistake: 5-min bias was Bullish while 1H/4H was Bearish — conflict signal. When 5-min is in strong bullish momentum INTO a resistance, the PSP break is more likely.

trades.lesson: When 5-min momentum is strongly Bullish and price approaches PSP with big green candles, the PSP is likely to break. Bias divergence (5min Bull vs HTF Bear) = lower probability short. Wait for actual rejection candle, not just arrival at level.

setup.trigger: TDO 29,572.5 sweep + brief reclaim — Claude called entry prematurely
setup.entry_reason: Sellside liquidity sweep below TDO 29,572.5 (low 29,536), price briefly recovered to 29,583

management.notes: Entry called too early — based on single quote reading at 29,583, not a 5-min candle close above TDO. Price never confirmed reclaim. Continued down to 29,511.
management.mistake: Claude called LONG on single price reading without waiting for 5-min candle CLOSE above TDO. Entry invalid — no candle confirmation. 1H/4H bias was BEARISH throughout.

trades.lesson: RULE: Only call entry after 5-min candle CLOSES above/below key level. Never on a single quote reading. TDO reclaim = 5-min close above 29,572.5, not just a touch.

setup.trigger: Short at FVG resistance — FVG not confirmed closed
setup.entry_reason: FVG overhead resistance + HTF bearish bias

management.notes: 3rd trade of the day — already had 2 losses (#4, #5). 5-min was in strong bullish momentum. FVG not confirmed. SL at 29,670 swept.
management.mistake: Entered short into strong 5-min bullish momentum. FVG had not confirmed closed. This was the 3rd trade after 2 losses — should have stopped after trade #5 (2-loss rule).

trades.lesson: RULE: Stop after 2 losses in a day. No exceptions. Also: shorting into consecutive large green candles = the level WILL break. Wait for actual rejection candle before entering.
